ground_truth.title: Vibe Coding Is Creating a Generation of Sorcerer's Apprentices
ground_truth.author: Russell Miller
ground_truth.main_content: There's a scene in Fantasia where Mickey Mouse, tired of carrying buckets of water, enchants a broom to do his chores. It works beautifully—until it doesn't. The broom splits into more brooms, each carrying more water, flooding the room. Mickey has no idea how to stop it because he never understood the magic in the first place. This is vibe coding in 2025. Vibe coding — the practice of describing what you want and letting an AI generate the code — has exploded. The developer has become a passenger in their own codebase. When you understand your code, you get: debugging capability, modification confidence, architectural intuition, and compound learning. Mentor Mode flips the relationship: Human describes goal → AI generates code AND teaches every concept → Human understands before moving on. In Vibe Mode, the AI is a wizard performing magic. In Mentor Mode, the AI is a teacher whose success is measured by whether the student can eventually work independently. Learning requires repetition. The goal isn't perpetual slowness. It's building the foundation that makes speed sustainable. Don't be Mickey. Try Mentor Mode.
with: The developer has become a passenger in their own codebase.
When you understand your code, you get: debugging capability, modification confidence, architectural intuition, and compound learning.
In Vibe Mode, the AI is a wizard performing magic. In Mentor Mode, the AI is a teacher whose success is measured by whether the student can eventually work independently.
Learning requires repetition. This is neuroscience, not opinion.
Don't be Mickey. Try Mentor Mode.
without: Thanks for reading! Subscribe for free to receive new posts and support my work.
